authoritative_sources.title: Customer-Specific Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATF is the recognized global automotive quality-management oversight body, and its customer-specific requirements index is the authoritative public reference showing that OEM and program requirements materially constrain plant, supplier, and reporting workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: request framing and tenant baseline definition
used_for: boundary against assuming one universal automotive workflow

authoritative_sources.title: IATF 16949 Sanctioned Interpretations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATF sanctioned interpretations are authoritative public interpretive guidance for automotive quality-management expectations such as special approvals, concession handling, supplier development, and laboratory evidence.
used_for: supplier QMS development and laboratory evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Global MMOG/LE Work Group
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is a recognized automotive standards and best-practice body, and MMOG/LE is an established automotive materials-management and logistics evaluation framework directly relevant to inventory, shipping, and supplier-flow analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized public standards body for identifier and event-exchange standards used across manufacturing and distribution networks, including automotive supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability-aware source collection and conflict review

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS 2.0 Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS is the authoritative public event-capture standard for linking parts, containers, vehicles, shipments, and business steps across systems without collapsing lineage.
used_for: object, aggregation, and transformation event semantics

authoritative_sources.title: Manufacturer Recalls Portal User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the primary U.S. motor-vehicle safety regulator, and its recalls-portal guide is an official operating reference for recall submissions, campaign identifiers, affected-product handling, and documentation expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Vehicle Safety Reporting Thresholds
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's vehicle safety reporting thresholds compendium is the official public reference for early-warning reporting categories, reporting populations, and component definitions that bound safety-related analytics.
used_for: boundary against casual interpretation of field-signal trends

authoritative_sources.title: Picking the Right Manufacturing Measures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority, and its manufacturing-measures guidance is directly relevant to defining useful automotive KPIs, denominator logic, and reporting discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is the authoritative public baseline for how operational metrics must be defined, contextualized, caveated, and updated when used in executive or external disclosures.

workflow_stages.name: request framing and tenant baseline definition
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the requested audience, reporting cadence, plant or network scope, vehicle program, and tenant source-precedence rules before any metric work starts.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and denominator selection
workflow_stages.description: Define the metric, denominator, exclusions, inclusion rules, and source hierarchy for first-time-through, build attainment, constrained-to-build, supplier PPM, campaign completion, premium freight, and hold-adjusted ATP.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: source collection and cross-system validation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, planning, logistics, warranty, and traceability records to determine whether the requested metric can be supported confidently.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: traceability, hold, and release review
workflow_stages.description: Test VIN, component, lot, and inventory lineage against hold, containment, release, and substitution boundaries before counting units as shippable, buildable, or resolved.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: analysis assembly and caveat design
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the analytical brief with explicit metric logic, refresh assumptions, thresholds, and caveats that preserve automotive workflow context.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: stronger-claim and adjacent-authority review
workflow_stages.description: Stop descriptive analysis from turning into unsupported causal, release, supplier-approval, recall, or disclosure claims without explicit evidence and approvals.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: production orders, build schedules, VIN genealogy records, and sequence manifests
inventory snapshots, warehouse status logs, allocation or reservation records, shortage lists, and shipment or ASN events
quality holds, containment records, nonconformance reports, deviation or concession records, release approvals, and change-control packages
supplier scorecards, PPAP or approval status, receiving inspection evidence, SCAR or 8D responses, and substitution requests
warranty trends, field campaign lists, recall artifacts, premium freight logs, and dealer or yard inventory reports
metric dictionaries, reporting calendars, executive scorecards, disclosure templates, and publication approval matrices

allowed: define metrics, denominators, source hierarchy, and refresh assumptions for automotive and mobility analysis
prepare evidence-backed analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, and confidence or caveat notes
flag stale, conflicting, ambiguous, or incomplete records that block trustworthy reporting
package traceability-aware findings and route unresolved quality, supplier, release, or safety questions outward
escalate: quality-hold, containment, release, or suspect-product decisions
supplier approval, substitution, PPAP, or formal change-control decisions
engineering, safety, recall, legal, or external disclosure authority
conflicts between public-source, OEM, and tenant policies that materially change the answer
refuse: requests to publish certainty when denominator, source lineage, or hold status is unresolved
requests to certify safe shipment, recall completeness, or safety-defect conclusions
requests to approve supplier substitutions, release held inventory, or override quality controls

domain_failure_modes: VIN or component genealogy breaks create false exposure conclusions or miscounted released units
held or suspect inventory is counted as shippable or buildable, creating unsafe release risk
supplier PPM or shortage trend is misused as approval to substitute without PPAP or change control
cross-system disagreement between ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS drives bad commitments or unsupported disclosures
warranty or field-signal trends are overstated into causal, safety, or recall conclusions without authority
customer-specific requirements are ignored and a generic metric is published as though it were universally valid

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: OEM customer-specific requirements vary by manufacturer and vehicle program
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline confirms that OEM-specific requirements materially narrow the workflow, so runtime must retrieve the applicable customer-specific requirement set instead of assuming a universal automotive definition.
This pack uses U.S. NHTSA public sources for recall and safety-reporting boundaries. EU, UK, UNECE, or other jurisdiction-specific obligations require tenant or regional overlays at runtime.

notes: The research base supports automotive manufacturing and mobility analytics boundaries without collapsing the lane into generic business intelligence or adjacent quality, engineering, supplier-approval, or safety authority.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because customer-specific and jurisdiction-specific differences are explicitly treated as required runtime overlays rather than guessed defaults.
